twitter.post: Ever feel like you are missing the stories that actually matter to you? The best way to stay informed is to find an app that learns your interests as you read. It makes keeping up with the news much easier. HEADLINNE.com #news #tech

linkedin.title: Most news apps try to guess what you like by showing you more of the same.
linkedin.body:
We decided to take a different approach with Headlinne. Instead of building a feed that just reflects your past clicks, we focused on building a system that understands the context of the news itself.

It is easy to get stuck in a loop where the algorithm only feeds you topics it knows you will click on. That is a quick way to narrow your worldview. We wanted to avoid that trap. So, we engineered our feed to prioritize variety and relevance over pure engagement metrics.

We treat the news as a connected web of information rather than just a list of headlines. By focusing on how stories relate to each other, we can surface important updates without burying you in repetitive content. It is a subtle shift in engineering, but it makes a big difference in how you experience the day's events.
linkedin.cta: If you are curious about a smarter way to stay informed, you can try it out at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Geofence warrants face new restrictions
slides.explanation: The Supreme Court has limited how the government can use geofence warrants to track location data. While they did not rule the practice unconstitutional, this is a significant step for digital privacy.

slides.headline: WhatsApp usernames are coming soon
slides.explanation: WhatsApp is rolling out usernames later this year. This update will let you chat with people without needing to share your actual phone number, keeping your personal details private.

slides.headline: Ford brings back human quality checks
slides.explanation: Ford decided to rehire human engineers after AI tools struggled to match the quality standards of their veteran technicians. It turns out human expertise is still hard to replace.

slides.headline: OpenAI teases new coding hardware
slides.explanation: OpenAI posted a video showing a small, square device with buttons. It seems to be related to their coding tool, Codex, and we can expect more details on July 15.

slides.headline: Google raises privacy concerns over EU rules
slides.explanation: The EU wants Google to share search data and open up its AI on Android. Google is pushing back, arguing that these changes could put user privacy at risk.

instagram.caption: From privacy updates to the limits of AI in car manufacturing, here is what you need to know in tech today. Keep up with the latest stories by following Headlinne. Which headline caught your eye? Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Supreme Court ruling on presidential power
slides.explanation: The Supreme Court ruled that the president now has more power to fire federal regulators. Liberal justices criticized the decision, saying it gives the president too much control.

slides.headline: Australian theatre industry in crisis
slides.explanation: Major musicals and operas are canceling shows due to rising costs. Experts say the industry needs tax reform to keep productions running and workers employed.

slides.headline: Trump must pay $5m in Carroll case
slides.explanation: The Supreme Court rejected a final appeal from Donald Trump regarding the E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case. He must now pay the $5 million in damages previously awarded.

slides.headline: Police hunt for 'Scorpio' letter writer
slides.explanation: Authorities are searching for the person behind over 100 threatening letters sent to Australian leaders since 2015. Police are asking for public help to solve the case.

slides.headline: Iran denies meeting with Trump
slides.explanation: Donald Trump claimed a meeting with Iran was happening in Doha today. Iranian officials have denied this, saying no such talks are planned.

instagram.caption: Stay up to date with today's top stories. From Supreme Court rulings to urgent calls for support in the arts, here is what you need to know. Download the Headlinne app for more daily updates. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
